steps.title: Find when the countries diverge most starkly
steps.content: The passage asserts the transition happened at “very different rates”—to support this claim with evidence, we need a year where France and the US are at markedly different stages of the same shift from agriculture to services.

steps.title: Match the data to the argument
steps.content:
By 1950, the divergence is unmistakable: the US had reached just 14% agriculture and 53% services, while France lingered at 32% agriculture and 35% services—clear proof of a much faster US transition.

The other three years fail to reveal this: 1800 and 1900 show both countries at similar transition stages, while 2012 shows them nearly converged.

A is correct.
